_comment: VA Pension (needs-based) and Survivors Pension MAPR rates. This is NOT disability compensation — see compensation.json for service-connected benefits. VA Pension is for wartime veterans (or surviving spouses) with limited income. Tax-free. Update annually each December when VA publishes new COLA rates.
_cola_note: 2.8% COLA effective December 1, 2025. VA Pension COLA matches Social Security COLA annually.

eligibility._comment: Summary of eligibility requirements. Not exhaustive — see VA.gov for full criteria.

veteran_pension.age_or_disability: Age 65+ OR permanently and totally disabled
veteran_pension.minimum_service: 90 days active duty with at least 1 day during wartime period (24 months for post-9/11 era)

survivors_pension.relationship: Unremarried surviving spouse (remarriage after age 57 permitted) OR unmarried dependent child
survivors_pension.marriage_duration: Married to veteran for at least 1 year before death, OR had a child together

wartime_periods.name: Gulf War
wartime_periods._note: End date TBD by Presidential proclamation or law

_net_worth_note: Effective Dec 1, 2025 through Nov 30, 2026. Net worth = assets + annual income. Primary residence and one vehicle excluded.
_lookback_note: VA reviews asset transfers in 3 years before claim. Transfers below fair market value may trigger penalty period of up to 5 years.
_additional_dependent_note: Add $2,984 to MAPR for each dependent beyond the first.
_medical_expense_note: Only unreimbursed medical expenses above 5% of applicable MAPR may be deducted from countable income.

veteran_pension_mapr._comment: Maximum Annual Pension Rate (MAPR) for veterans. Pension = MAPR minus countable income. All values annual USD.

two_veterans_married._comment: Both spouses are qualified veterans married to each other.

survivors_pension_mapr._comment: Maximum Annual Pension Rate (MAPR) for surviving spouses and children. All values annual USD.

surviving_child_only._note: Qualified surviving child with no surviving parent.

survivors_pension_mapr._sbp_miw_note: Survivor Benefit Plan / Minimum Income Widow limitation amount.

_how_pension_calculated: Annual Pension = MAPR - Countable Income. Countable income includes earnings, SS, retirement payments, investment income (veteran + dependents). Reduced by unreimbursed medical expenses above 5% of MAPR. If countable income >= MAPR, no pension payable. If zero income, pension = full MAPR.
_tax_treatment: VA Pension is completely tax-free. Does not count as income for federal or state income tax purposes.
_medicaid_interaction_note: Basic Pension counts toward Medicaid income limits in most states. A&A and Housebound portions are exempt in most states. Nursing Home Medicaid beneficiaries may have pension reduced to $90/month. Medicaid requires applicants to apply for VA pension if eligible.
